rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scopes; they are only allowed in component types.
When assigning values in implementation contracts, ensure the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, always resolve references to visible AADL features or declared helpers before using them in expressions.
Use assign statements only for valid output features or declared auxiliary variables in implementation-level contracts.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scopes; move them to component type scopes.
When a diagnostic reports '<error>' type, trace back to the first unresolved identifier on the same line and resolve it.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE guarantee statements must be placed in component type scopes, not implementation scopes.
Assign statements in implementation contracts must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Helper declarations in AGREE must be visible in the same annex or refer to valid AADL features.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to reference undefined identifiers or use '<error>' types in AGREE expressions without resolving their source.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation-level component scopes; they are only valid in component types.
Do not add new architecture elements to resolve AGREE type mismatches; instead, correct the root cause or remove invalid clauses.
